source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to BOP/LMRP

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Green Canyon Block 243

actions_taken: ROV backed away from stack and inspected BOP stack and riser
BSEE Houma District contacted and recovery plans initiated
Riser adapter sent to Stress Engineering Services for analysis
Finite element analysis performed on riser adapter
event.summary: On January 14, 2019, during temporary abandonment operations on well SS002 aboard the Seadrill Sevan Louisiana, the riser adapter failed and allowed the BOP stack to fall to the seafloor. The incident occurred when the Subsea Supervisor reduced riser tension to 440 KIPS instead of the agreed 650 KIPS while unlatching the wellhead connector. This reduced tension caused the BOP stack to tilt approximately 45 degrees, placing excessive bending loads on the riser adapter. The riser adapter partially ruptured immediately and fully failed 17 hours later, causing the BOP stack to fall. The well was already secured with multiple plugs and cement, preventing pollution.
recommendations: BSEE Houma District recommends issuing a Safety Alert to raise awareness about utilizing proper rig procedures when disconnecting
Seadrill implemented preventative action to limit all rigs to 50 KIPS when disconnecting from wells
Operators should establish safe working parameters for setting down weight by slacking off riser tensioners
Engineering analysis and simulation should be performed to determine maximum and minimum safe set down weights

hazards.name: High-pressure subsea environment with suspended BOP stack
Riser system structural integrity under compression

threats.name: Inadequate riser tension during wellhead connector unlatch
Riser adapter structural failure due to excessive bending loads
BOP stack tilt and loss of stability on wellhead

consequences.name: Equipment damage and loss of containment capability

controls.name: Riser tensioner system with predetermined set down weight
controls.barrier_role: Maintain adequate tension on riser to prevent BOP stack tilt during wellhead connector unlatch
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent excessive compression and tilting

supporting_text: The Subsea Supervisor adjusted the tensioners from the electrical technician's office located on the drill floor
The top tension was reduced to approximately 440 KIPS instead of 650 KIPS as had been agreed
With the top tension reduced to 440 KIPS, the FE models documented that tilting of the BOP stack was the likely outcome

controls.name: Procedure for setting predetermined weight down on BOP stack
controls.barrier_role: Provide clear operational guidance for safe riser tension parameters
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure consistent safe operating parameters

supporting_text: The procedure simply stated to 'set a pre-determined weight down on the stack by slacking off the tensioners,' and did not provide a specific weight
Poor procedure. The procedure did not state a specific weight to be applied by slacking off the riser tensioners
The rig was given free will to determine the set down weight, and there was no known engineering analysis and/or simulation performed

controls.name: ROV inspection and monitoring during wellhead connector unlatch
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal BOP stack behavior and prevent further damage
controls.lod_basis: Detection and intervention to prevent catastrophic failure

supporting_text: The ROV backed away from the stack and inspected the BOP stack and riser
The ROV observed the BOP stack was off the wellhead, barely resting on top of the connection and found the riser adapter partially ruptured
The ROV flew around to the opposite side for verification of the function, when unexpectedly the BOPs tilted approximately 45 degrees

controls.name: Well security with bridge plugs and cement
controls.barrier_role: Prevent loss of containment and environmental pollution if BOP stack fails
controls.lod_basis: Final barrier to prevent well control incident and pollution

supporting_text: The well was secured via multiple plugs prior to the BOP's falling and no pollution was attributed to this incident
The surface bridge plug was set at 3,330 feet with cement placed on top and pressure tested as permitted by BSEE

controls.name: Engineering analysis and finite element modeling of riser system
controls.barrier_role: Establish safe operating parameters based on structural analysis
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to determine safe working limits

supporting_text: There was no known engineering analysis and/or simulation performed to aid the operational personnel to determine a maximum or minimum set down weight
The lack of analysis into safe parameters for setting down weight by slacking off the riser tensioners allowed substantial errors to be made

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
